_comment: Python project hooks example. Copy to your project's .claude/settings.json
_usage: Place this content in your project's .claude/settings.json file

hooks.command: FILE_PATH="$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH"; if [ -n "$FILE_PATH" ] && echo "$FILE_PATH" | grep -q '\.py$'; then cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)"; if [ -f "pyproject.toml" ] && command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1; then uv run ruff check --fix "$FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; uv run ruff format "$FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; uv run mypy "$FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; elif command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1; then ruff check --fix "$FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; ruff format "$FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; if command -v mypy >/dev/null 2>&1; then mypy "$FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; fi; fi; fi; exit 0

FILE_PATH=$(echo '$TOOL_INPUT' | grep -o '"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"[^"]*"' | sed 's/.*"\([^"]*\)"$/\1/' 2>/dev/null); PROTECTED='.env .secret credentials pyproject.toml uv.lock private_key'; for p in $PROTECTED; do case "$FILE_PATH" in *$p*) echo "BLOCKED: Cannot edit protected file: $FILE_PATH" >&2; exit 2;; esac; done; exit 0
